articles.title: The Needs of Yesterday
articles.author: Bertha A. Kleinman
articles.complete_text:
Unbowed of years nor yet of pungent sorrow,
Unsmarted of doubt that probes its avid way,
I yet have need of grace and a tomorrow
To right the many wrongs of yesterday.

The many hours I have not to filling.
The wasted days of empty little wiles,
The crying tasks that have not found me willing
The span of cloud that needed but my smiles;

The fleeting beats of chance so easy squandered,
The thousand little precious waits of time —
And all the ways of quest I have not wandered,
Where timid ones might cling to hands of mine.

Ah me! the little zest and care expended.
The meager, little, momentary zeal —
To others' wrongs that might have been amended.
And rasping little hurts that I could heal!

O Thou who knowest every throb of sorrow.
Bear still with me, for I would know the way,
That somewhere in the might of Thy tomorrow,
I may amend the needs of yesterday!
articles.notes: This is a complete poem by Bertha A. Kleinman that appears in the misc content but should be extracted as a standalone piece. It's a reflective poem about regrets and seeking redemption.

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of magazine front matter including advertisements for various products (Superfulo hair removal, Royal Bread, Columbia Grafonola, etc.), table of contents for September 1920 issue, advertisers' directory, General Board member listings, and back matter with more advertisements and subscription information. There's also a partial biographical piece about Mrs. May Wright Sewell that appears to be a fragment from a larger article already extracted.
